summary: Iteration 21: Retest of 2 previously failing bugs. Both bugs confirmed FIXED. All 5 required test scenarios passing.
passed_tests: Backend: /api/articles/{id} returns correct article with price=1500 (nested under 'article' key)
Backend: GET /api/articles/{article_id}/validate-scope returns valid=true and price=1500
Backend: POST /api/orders/preview WITHOUT _scope_unlock returns is_scope_request=True, subtotal=0
Backend: POST /api/orders/preview WITH _scope_unlock returns is_scope_request=False, subtotal=1500 - FIXED!
Backend: POST /api/orders/preview summary.one_time.subtotal=1500 and summary.scope_request.count=0 after unlock
Backend: POST /api/orders/scope-request creates order with notes_json
Backend: scope_request order has notes_json.product_intake, notes_json.payment, notes_json.system_metadata
Backend: notes_json.product_intake contains the correct product_id key
Frontend: Single TopNav on /articles page - [data-testid='top-nav'] count=1 (was 2 before fix)
Frontend: Single TopNav on /articles/:id page - [data-testid='top-nav'] count=1 (was 2 before fix)
Frontend: Product page scope ID input (scope-id-input) visible and functional
Frontend: Scope unlock validates and shows green success box with title 'Test Scope Final' and $1500
Frontend: CTA button text shows 'Add to cart — $1500' after scope unlock
Frontend: Add to cart navigates to /cart with item in One-time purchases section at $1500
Frontend: Cart page shows 'One-time purchases' section with Fixed-Scope Development at $1500.00
Frontend: Cart 'Scope n Pay Later' section shows 'No scope requests yet.' (empty - correct!)
Frontend: Card Payment (Stripe) selection shows 5% fee, total $1575.00
Frontend: Checkout with scope-unlocked product creates Stripe session - redirects to checkout.stripe.com at US$1,575.00
critical_code_review_comments: INFO: scope_request_form endpoint (/api/orders/scope-request-form) does not include notes_json in the order document (unlike /api/orders/scope-request which does). This may be acceptable since it's a different flow (scope form, not cart checkout). Consider adding notes_json for consistency.
INFO: The validate-scope endpoint is GET /api/articles/{article_id}/validate-scope (no product_id parameter). This is fine.
INFO: CartContext uses localStorage for persistence. Important for testing - page.goto() in Playwright creates fresh navigation but localStorage persists across same-domain navigations.
seed_data_creation: Created and deleted 1 test scope_request order during notes_json test. DB is clean.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both bugs from iteration 20 are now confirmed fixed: (1) Double TopNav on /articles and /articles/:id is fixed - only 1 TopNav visible. (2) Scope unlock cart price is fixed - scope-unlocked item shows as one-time purchase at $1500, not scope_request at $0. Stripe checkout flow also works correctly at $1575 (=$1500 + 5% fee). Backend 9/9 tests in /app/backend/tests/test_bug_retest_iter21.py all pass. No new issues found.

rca_confirmed.double_topnav_fix: Confirmed fixed: /articles and /articles/:articleId routes moved OUTSIDE of <BaseLayout> in App.tsx (same level as /store and /product/:productId). Both pages now render exactly 1 TopNav.
rca_confirmed.scope_unlock_price_fix: Confirmed fixed: calculate_price() in server.py now checks inputs.get('_scope_unlock') for scope_request products. When present with a price, returns is_scope_request=False, requires_checkout=True, and uses scope_unlock.price as subtotal. CartItemInput also has price_override field. Full E2E flow works: validate scope -> add to cart at $1500 -> cart shows one-time purchase -> Stripe checkout at $1575.
